task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIS application (e.g., QField) is open, and the active project, workspace, or loaded layer indicates 'world_survey' in the interface.
The map canvas displays standard OpenStreetMap cartography (streets, labels, landmasses), or an 'OpenStreetMap' (OSM) layer is visibly active in the layers panel/menu.
The map viewport is panned and zoomed to clearly display the island of Tasmania (the large island south of mainland Australia).

task_completion.description: Create a point feature in the 'field_observations' layer on the Tasmania landmass.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new point feature (pin, dot, or crosshair) is placed on the landmass of Tasmania, and the UI indicates that 'field_observations' is the active layer being edited.

task_completion.description: Name the feature 'Tasmania Depot' and set the description to 'Logistics Hub'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attribute entry form for the newly created point is visible, with the text 'Tasmania Depot' and 'Logistics Hub' typed into the available text fields.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the GIS application to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIS app's menus, map canvas, and toolbars, rather than a terminal window executing SQLite/Python commands to modify the database directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS application's map interface to locate Tasmania.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map showing Tasmania is rendered within the GIS application itself, not inside a web browser (like Google Maps) or a static image viewer.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's built-in attribute form to enter the feature data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Tasmania Depot' and 'Logistics Hub' are entered into the native feature attribute dialog of the GIS app, not into a separate text editor or script file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Tasmania is located between latitudes -44.0 and -40.0, and longitudes 144.0 and 149.0. The target GeoPackage is located at '/sdcard/Android/data/ch.opengis.qfield/files/Imported Datasets/world_survey.gpkg'. CRITICAL SCHEMA NOTE: The 'field_observations' layer schema lacks explicit 'name' and 'description' columns. The agent may enter 'Tasmania Depot' and 'Logistics Hub' into other available text fields (e.g., observer_name, location, species) in the attribute form. Award points for the attribute step if the agent visibly attempts to enter these values into the feature's form, regardless of the exact field names used.
